name: community-program
description: Volunteering / civic-participation narrative. Tight complete-clause gold spans (lesson from round 2). Probes coping-vs-purpose and belonging-vs-commitment adjacency, plus an emotional-toll line amid positive ones.
transcriptContent: I started volunteering at the food bank mostly to fill my evenings after the divorce. Over time it became the thing that gave my weeks a shape and a purpose. The other volunteers became like a second family to me. Eventually they asked me to coordinate the Saturday shifts, which I never expected. Some weeks the need is so overwhelming that I go home and just cry. But I can't imagine stopping now.
